## Summary
This repository contains code for a Python app.
It converts .csv files generated by Daylio - an Android/iOS journaling app - into markdown .md files that can be stored in an Obsidian vault.
Obsidian is a markdown-based note-taking app that allows you to create a personal knowledge base.

## Terminology
* (journal) entry - a single journal entry in Daylio. It has the following properties:
  * date - the date of the entry
    * date is mandatory
  * mood - the mood of the entry (e.g. "happy", "sad", "angry", etc.)
    * mood is mandatory
    * mood can either come from a default list of moods in the Daylio app
    * or it can be a custom mood that the user defines in a `.json` file for the converter
  * activities - the activities that the user did on that day
    * activities are optional
    * you pick activities from a list in the Daylio app. You can customise the activities in the list however you like.
  * notes - the notes that the user wrote on that day
    * notes are optional
    * notes can contain any text, including emojis and special characters
    * you can choose to write your note in two ways:
      * a simple raw text note without any formatting (similar to str)
      * a note with some rich formatting (e.g. bold, italic, lists, etc.) that is written in markdown syntax internally
      * the note with rich formatting can also have an optional title as well
* group - a group of entries written on the same day

## Architecture
The converter is a single module with a sub-module `entry` for interpreting the moods.

### Input: Daylio .csv
```csv
full_date,date,weekday,time,mood,activities,scales,note_title,note
2026-04-01,April 1,Wednesday,18:00,vaguely good,"outdoors | volleyball","","","<b>test</b><br><br>test2<br><br>test3"
```

#### Scales
Note that `scales` have been recently introduced in Daylio.
Scales are a different "kind" of activity. Instead of a single string, scales can be:
* numerical - ranging from any minimum value to a maximum value (0-10, for example). The min and max value is user-defined
* categorical - a list of user-defined categories (e.g. "low", "medium", "high", etc.)
* time - a time duration (e.g. "1 hour", "30 minutes", etc.). The user picks a duration from an Android time picker.
